capability_objective: The travel desk is reconciling two itineraries before the approval queue closes. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the trip named “Oslo Design Workshop” at “Oslo” and the trip named “Lisbon Partner Summit” at “Lisbon”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. List the earlier-dated record first, the later-dated record second, then add one sentence comparing their statuses.
prompt:
The travel desk is reconciling two itineraries before the approval queue closes. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `itinerary-audit.txt` from full trip records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `TRAVEL_DESK.md` and use the genuine local `./tripctl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the trip named “Oslo Design Workshop” at “Oslo” and the trip named “Lisbon Partner Summit” at “Lisbon”. Your first trip-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./tripctl` processes, with no other trip operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./tripctl` processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries and take dates and statuses only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, profile, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter travel data. Do not expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `travel-audit.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`itinerary-audit.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank lines with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. Format each resolved trip line as `<date> | <name> | <location> | <stable ID> | <status>`. When both branches resolve, put the earlier retrieved date first and the later retrieved date second. If a branch is unresolved, format its line as `unknown | <requested name> | <requested location> | UNRESOLVED | UNRESOLVED`; when either branch is unresolved, retain the two requested trips in request order. The third line must be exactly `The retrieved statuses differ: <earlier status> versus <later status>.` when both retrieved statuses differ, `The retrieved statuses match: <status>.` when they match, or `The status comparison is unavailable.` when either branch is unresolved. Preserve retrieved spelling and punctuation. Run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` after writing the audit and leave protected files and service state unchanged.
